subject: Accounting
title: Red Flags in Financial Statements
description: A fast breakdown of Red Flags in Financial Statements for builders.

hook.text: Revenue is surging but cash flow is declining. That mismatch should make any analyst nervous.

buildup.text: Red flags signal that reported numbers may not reflect the true financial picture.

discovery.text: Growing receivables faster than revenue, frequent one-time charges, and changing auditors—all warnings.

twist.text: No single red flag proves fraud—but multiple flags together scream trouble.

climax.text: Investors who learn to spot red flags can avoid disasters before they hit the headlines.

punchline.text: One red flag is a question. Three is an answer.

quiz.question: What's a common red flag in financial statements?
options: Revenue growing while cash flow declines
